title: Maharashtra monsoon news: When will it rain in Mumbai, Pune, Nagpur, Ratnagiri, Jalgaon, Amravati, Thane, Nashik? Relief from heat likely from…; check full IMD forecast - news24online.com
full_text:
The southwest monsoon has slowed its progress across Maharashtra, bringing disappointment to residents who have been waiting for relief from the intense summer heat. According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the monsoon has advanced up to the Harne-Dapoli region but has not moved further due to the lack of favourable atmospheric conditions.
As a result, large parts of the state, including Mumbai, continue to experience hot and humid weather.
Mumbai continues to sizzle
Mumbai has been witnessing uncomfortable weather conditions, with daytime temperatures remaining well above normal levels. The city recorded a maximum temperature of 36 degrees Celsius, around 3.5 degrees Celsius higher than the seasonal average.
In the suburbs, Santacruz registered a maximum temperature of 35.2 degrees Celsius, while the minimum temperature remained elevated at 30.2 degrees Celsius, adding to the discomfort caused by high humidity levels.
Despite the delay in monsoon advancement, the weather department has indicated that rainfall activity could begin increasing across Maharashtra from next week.
El Niño may influence rainfall pattern
Weather experts have attributed the sluggish monsoon progress partly to the strengthening El Niño conditions in the Pacific Ocean.
Meteorologists say El Niño could affect rainfall distribution across India this season. While some regions may experience delayed or below-normal rainfall, others could witness intense spells of rain over short durations.
Heatwave conditions grip several regions
Maharashtra continues to remain under the influence of severe heat, with temperatures soaring across multiple districts.
The Vidarbha region remained the hottest part of the state. Akola and Wardha recorded maximum temperatures of 43.5 degrees Celsius, while Yavatmal touched 43.2 degrees Celsius. Amravati reported 42.4 degrees Celsius and Nagpur recorded 41 degrees Celsius.
Several districts in Marathwada and Central Maharashtra also saw temperatures ranging between 39 degrees Celsius and 42 degrees Celsius.
Pune recorded a maximum temperature of 35.9 degrees Celsius, which was 3.5 degrees Celsius above normal, while the minimum temperature settled at 26.1 degrees Celsius.
Other major temperature readings included:
Coastal areas also warmer than usual
Above-normal temperatures were also reported along Maharashtra’s coastline.
Alibag recorded 37.2 degrees Celsius, while Dahanu registered 36.8 degrees Celsius. Ratnagiri recorded a maximum temperature of 33.5 degrees Celsius.
The persistent heat has increased concerns among residents as the expected monsoon relief remains delayed.
No significant rainfall recorded
According to IMD data, no rainfall was recorded across Konkan-Goa, Central Maharashtra, Marathwada and Vidarbha during the observation period.
Pune continues to face a significant seasonal rainfall deficit, with a shortfall of 62.3 mm compared to the normal rainfall expected by this time of year. The situation has raised concerns among farmers and local authorities who are closely monitoring weather developments.
IMD advises precaution
The weather department has urged people to take precautions against heat-related illnesses. Citizens have been advised to stay hydrated, avoid prolonged exposure to direct sunlight during afternoon hours and take necessary measures to protect themselves from extreme heat.
Meanwhile, meteorologists continue to closely track the monsoon’s progress, as evolving El Niño conditions are expected to play a crucial role in shaping rainfall patterns during the season.
